title: dampiera incana var. incana
intro: Dampiera incana var. incana, also known as the grey dampiera, is a shrub-like plant native to Western Australia, belonging to the family Goodeniaceae. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Dampiera incana var. incana include its potential use in treating wounds, skin infections, and respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma. The plant can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or topical ointment, and it is also used in traditional medicine as a treatment for various ailments.
